title: Marketing Manager (Partnership)
description:
Grounded in a talent-centric mindset, Maker Lab is a marketing agency designed to build decentralised technology-led agency teams, rooted in collaboration with clients from the ground up. Maker Lab s cross-functional teams of multidisciplinary specialists are embedded into client teams across digital marketing competencies from measurement analytics to content production. 
 Role Summary: 
 We are looking for a creative architect execution engine for high-priority marketing brand partnerships. Shifts focus on long-term, value-multiplying alliances. Responsibilities include end-to-end campaign lifecycle, from C-suite pitching opportunity evaluation to leading execution, internal navigation, and performance reporting. 
 Role: 
 Lead the intake and initial vetting of all inbound marketing partnership opportunities against criteria including strategic alignment, brand fit, commercial viability, and policy risk. 
 Perform rigorous due diligence on potential partners, culminating in executive-ready go/no-go recommendation briefs for senior stakeholders. 
 Manage the Centre of Excellences (CoE) master partnership pipeline, ensuring real-time status visibility and facilitating defined decision-making cycles. 
 Drive continuous market landscaping across relevant consumer sectors to proactively identify and source high-potential co-marketing and brand alliance targets. 
 Collaborate with internal product marketing teams to align organisational priorities with targeted outbound partnership strategies. 
 Design and champion tailored, integrated brand solutions and joint co-marketing value propositions in partnership with creative, media, and agency teams. 
 Create high-impact, visually compelling pitch decks, proposals, and strategic briefs for internal review and external C-suite engagement. 
 Own the execution of finalised marketing partnerships, converting high-level agreements into comprehensive campaign plans with defined milestones, assets, timelines, and ownership. 
 Serve as the Single Point of Contact (SPOC) for external partner marketing and agency execution teams. 
 Coordinate execution across complex internal organisational structures, engaging cross-functional teams (Product Marketing, Brand, Creative, PR, Comms, Legal, and Media) to accelerate campaign velocity. 
 Enforce strict adherence to all internal brand guidelines, visual identity standards, tone of voice, and policy guardrails across all public-facing partner assets. 
 Proactively manage risks, identifying and resolving delivery bottlenecks, brand integrity concerns, or legal hurdles prior to public launch. 
 Develop and maintain campaign tracking dashboards, providing concise and structured progress updates (e.g., milestones achieved, tasks at risk) to senior leadership. 
 Collaborate with insights and measurement teams to deploy accurate tracking frameworks, brand lift studies, and key performance indicators (KPIs) pre-launch. 
 Lead structured post-delivery reviews, compiling detailed debriefs that analyse actual vs. target ROI, overall performance, audience insights, and actionable recommendations for future strategy. 
 Directly contribute to the CoE s operational playbook, standard operating procedures (SOPs), and standardised templates to build long-term, scalable institutional marketing knowledge. 
 You: 
 BA/BS degree or equivalent experience 
 6+ years of expertise in marketing partnerships, brand management, account management, brand solutions, or integrated campaign program management, specifically within the Indian market. 
 Proven ability to lead complex, cross-functional projects, fostering consensus and driving execution across highly matrixed internal teams (including Product Marketing, Brand, Creative, PR, Comms, Legal, and Policy) in the absence of direct line authority. 
 Profound understanding of the Indian consumer internet market, digital media landscape, marketing ecosystems, and nuanced consumer behaviour across various audience segments. 
 Experience collaborating with or direct experience at a major media or creative agency/network. 
 Demonstrated ability to navigate and ensure compliance with data privacy policies, brand safety guidelines, and visual identity standards throughout the campaign lifecycle. 
 Fluency in marketing metrics and campaign Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) (e.g., reach, engagement, brand lift, ad recall), with a track record of translating performance data into strategic and actionable insights. 
 Exceptional verbal and written English communication and presentation skills.

title: Creator Marketing Manager
description:
Role: Creator Marketing Manager 
 Location: Gurgaon, India 
 About the role: 
 As the Creator Marketing Manager for Google India s social channels, you are the voice of Google to millions of Indians. You wont just be "writing copy" you ll be building the narrative of how India uses technology to dream, learn, and grow. From high-production brand campaigns to real-time "trend-jacking" and collaborating with India s top creators, you will ensure Google India remains the most helpful and loved brand on the Indian internet. 
 What you will be doing: 
 Building the creator infrastructure for the team - identifying, brand safety checks, briefing - end to end process. 
 The Voice of Google India: Define and execute the creative tone for Google India across YouTube Shorts, Instagram, X, and more. You ll turn "product features" into "cultural moments." 
 Social Storytelling: Lead the end-to-end creation of social-first content from scripting "day in the life" creator videos to conceptualizing interactive stories about Google Maps or Search trends. 
 Trend-Jacking Real-Time Content: Monitor the Indian cultural pulse (Cricket, Bollywood, exams, festivals) and find authentic ways for Google to join the conversation. 
 Creator Collaboration: Partner with Indias leading influencers and creators to co-create content that feels like a natural part of their feed, not a forced ad. 
 Creative Directing Agencies: Lead and inspire our agency partners, ensuring their output is "social-native" and meets Google s high bar for craft and empathy. 
 Stakeholder Navigation: Work with global product teams and local marketing leads to translate complex business goals into simple, delightful social content. 
 Budget Production Ownership: Manage the lifecycle of social productions, from annual agency contracts to specific campaign budgets. 
 What you need to be great in this role: 
 8+ years of experience in digital marketing, social media agencies, or content platform preferably in the creator space 
 A portfolio that showcases social-first thinking (not just print ads or TVCs). 
 Experience managing large-scale brand social handles. 
 Exceptional writing skills in English (fluency in Hindi or other Indian languages is a massive plus). 
 Platform Mastery: Deep expertise in YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels ecosystems. 
 The "Script-to-Screen" Skill: Experience in directing or producing short-form video content. 
 Creator Network: Existing knowledge of or relationships within the Indian creator economy. 
 Analytical Mindset: The ability to look at a Google Trends dashboard or social analytics and say, "There s a story here." 
 Deep understanding of the creator ecosystem 
 Cultural Intuition: You have a deep love for Indian internet culture and understand the nuances of different regions, languages, and behaviors. 
 The "Maker" Mindset: You aren t afraid to pick up a phone to shoot a demo or jump into a rough edit to show a vision. You think in "hooks" and "retention." 
 Strategic Creativity: You dont just ideate for "likes"; you ideate to build brand love and solve user problems. 
 Collaboration Skills: You can present a bold, quirky idea to a room of corporate stakeholders and get them excited about it. 
 Operational Excellence: You can manage multiple workstreams (Always-on vs. Big Campaigns) without breaking a sweat.

title: Head of D2C Growth
description:
We are looking for a high-agency operator to act as the "mini-CEO" of our D2C engine, taking full strategic and execution ownership over our entire Shopify, growth marketing, and analytics ecosystem. 
 If you are a data-driven builder who thrives on moving fast, scaling budgets, and running the P&L of a channel like your own startup, this is for you. 
 Skip the corporate bureaucracycome partner directly with leadership to pioneer and own our next phase of hyper-growth. 
 Job Description 
 Job Title : Head of D2C Growth
 Location : HSR Layout, Bangalore 
 Experience : 5-8 years 
 Employment Type : Full-time 
 Role Overview: 
 Were looking for a strategic and execution-focused Senior Manager D2C to lead end-to-end growth across our D2C channel. This role will own both e-commerce operations and performance marketing, driving customer acquisition, retention, and revenue. 
 You will be responsible for managing website performance, paid media, data analytics, and customer lifecycle while ensuring a seamless, high-converting user journey. The ideal candidate combines strong analytical thinking with creativity and a deep understanding of consumer behavior. 
 Key Responsibilities: 
 1. D2C Website & Operations 
 Manage end-to-end Shopify operations including product listings, collections, pricing, and offers 
 Oversee order fulfillment, inventory sync, RTO reduction, and operational efficiency 
 Ensure smooth website functionality, UX, and conversion optimization 
 2. Performance Marketing & Acquisition 
 Own and scale paid acquisition across Meta (Facebook, Instagram) and Google (Search, Display, YouTube, Shopping) 
 Build and execute full-funnel strategies (prospecting, retargeting, retention) 
 Manage budgets, CAC, ROAS, and revenue targets aligned with business goals 
 3. Campaign Execution & Optimization 
 Launch, monitor, and optimize campaigns for performance and scale 
 Run A/B tests across ads, audiences, creatives, and landing pages 
 Continuously improve targeting, bidding strategies, and funnel performance 
 4. Data, Analytics & Reporting 
 Track key D2C and marketing metrics (traffic, CVR, CAC, ROAS, LTV, AOV) 
 Build dashboards and share daily/weekly performance insights 
 Ensure accurate tracking via GA4, pixels, and conversion APIs 
 Drive data-backed decision-making across teams 
 5. Customer Experience & Retention 
 Optimize on-site journey and checkout funnel to reduce drop-offs 
 Work closely with CRM to execute retention strategies (WhatsApp, Email, SMS) 
 Improve repeat purchase rate and customer lifetime value 
 6. Creative & Cross-functional Collaboration 
 Partner with creative, tech, and marketing teams for high-impact campaigns 
 Provide data-backed briefs for ad creatives, landing pages, and PDPs 
 Ensure brand consistency and conversion-focused communication 
 7. Growth & Strategy 
 Drive revenue through pricing strategies, bundling, and offer planning 
 Identify new growth levers, channels, and experimentation opportunities 
 Benchmark competitors and stay updated with market trends 
 What Were Looking For: 
 5–8 years of experience in D2C/e-commerce + performance marketing 
 Strong hands-on experience with Meta Ads and Google Ads 
 Deep understanding of metrics like CAC, ROAS, LTV, MER, and AOV 
 Experience with Shopify, GA4, Google Tag Manager, and CRM tools (Klaviyo or similar) 
 Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills 
 Ability to balance data with creativity and consumer understanding 
 Preferred (Good to Have): 
 Experience in beauty, skincare, or consumer brands 
 Exposure to app marketing, marketplaces, or omnichannel growth 
 Experience leading teams or managing cross-functional stakeholders 
 What We Offer: 
 Competitive salary and benefits 
 High ownership and fast growth environment 
 Opportunity to build and scale a high-impact D2C + growth function

title: Head of Growth & Revenue Operations
description:
The Role 
 As Head of Revenue & Operations, you own two things simultaneously: the performance marketing engine that fills the top of the funnel, and the inside sales floor that converts it into revenue. This is not a strategy role and not a single-channel specialist role it is an end-to-end commercial ownership role.
 You will report directly to the CEO and lead teams spanning paid acquisition, sales operations, analytics, and retention. Your mandate is to build a profitable, scalable revenue system powered by data, AI, and tight cross-functional execution with zero tolerance for vanity metrics.
 Key Responsibilities 
 PPC & Performance Marketing 
 Own all paid media channels — Google Ads, Meta, and emerging platforms — with full ROAS and CAC accountability
 Build and manage full-funnel campaign architecture: ToFu awareness through BoFu retargeting and remarketing
 Drive bidding strategy evolution: from Max Clicks Max Conversions tCPA/tROAS as data matures
 Own attribution modelling — build a reliable multi-touch model that informs spend allocation across channels
 Run structured A/B and creative testing frameworks across landing pages, ad creatives, and audience segments
 Stay ahead of platform changes: PMax, Demand Gen, AI-powered bidding, and new Google/Meta features
 Leverage AI tools for audience segmentation, dynamic creative, predictive bidding, and content generation
 Build and own dashboards that give leadership a real-time view of acquisition efficiency — not vanity metrics
 Inside Sales Operations 
 Drive weekly and monthly revenue targets from existing inside sales funnels with Sales GM .
 Ensure TL performance, coaching quality, and development — upgrade or exit weak supervisors
 Own RCA for all operational failures: FRT misses, conversion drops, follow-up leakage, productivity loss
 Design and enforce incentive structures that drive the right behaviours and prevent gaming
 Own capacity planning, attrition management, PIPs, and quality of incoming hires
 Implement CRM hygiene, SLA enforcement, and feedback loops between marketing and sales
 Protect CEO bandwidth — zero operational escalations should reach the founder
 Analytics, MIS & Attribution 
 Build a single source of truth for Sales, Marketing, and Product teams via unified dashboards
 Define and track north-star KPIs: ROAS, CAC, Opportunity Loss %, Unassisted Conversion Rate, Retention Rate
 Own data pipelines and reporting infrastructure — SQL, Big Query, or equivalent tools preferred
 Translate data into clear narratives for leadership — connect numbers to decisions, not just reports
 Retention & Lifecycle 
 Build lifecycle marketing programmes — email, WhatsApp, and CRM-driven reactivation flows
 Run cohort analysis to identify high-LTV segments and reduce churn in at-risk cohorts
 Own repeat purchase rate as a KPI — build referral and loyalty loops that reduce CAC over time
 AI-First Execution 
 Actively deploy AI tools across the growth stack: personalisation, lead scoring, content generation, dynamic pricing
 Champion a culture of experimentation and rapid iteration using AI-augmented workflows
 Evaluate and onboard new AI tooling — build internal capability, not just vendor dependency
 Requirements 
 Experience 
 8–14 years of overall experience across growth marketing, PPC, and/or revenue operations
 Minimum 4 years of hands-on PPC ownership — Google Ads and Meta — with direct ROAS accountability
 Minimum 3 years managing a B2C inside sales team with direct revenue targets
 Demonstrated track record of owning a P&L or revenue number end-to-end
 D2C or e-commerce background strongly preferred — high-AOV categories a bonus
 Technical Skills 
 Deep fluency in Google Ads: campaign structures, bidding logic, attribution, PMax, Demand Gen
 Meta Ads: creative testing frameworks, audience strategy, ROAS optimisation
 MIS & Analytics: dashboarding, SQL or BigQuery preferred, Power BI / Looker / GA4
 CRM platforms: LeadSquared, Salesforce, Zoho, or equivalent
 AI tools: actively using AI in current workflow — not just awareness
 Education 
 Graduation or Post-graduation from Tier 1 colleges accepted
 Strong analytical aptitude & Quantitative knowledge.
 Soft Skills & Mindset 
 Highly analytical yet action-oriented — comfortable making decisions with imperfect data
 Scrappy builder mindset: does more with less, not dependent on large teams or big budgets
 Excellent cross-functional leader — can align product, engineering, sales, and marketing to shared goals
 Strong communication: can translate data into narrative for founders and floor equally
 Ownership language throughout — accountable for outcomes, not activities

title: Head of Brand and Growth
description:
Head of Brand & Growth signals senior ownership of both the narrative and the numbers — filtering for candidates who refuse the brand-vs-performance false choice this role is built to resolve. 
 ROLE SNAPSHOT 
 You are the custodian of the full group’s brand identity — Eden Courtyard, The Green Room, Dulaar, Backyard Chills, and OneShot Turf — across premium dining, casual dining, mithai, and sports retail. You hold two mandates at once: protect the distinct voice of each brand (they are siblings, never twins, and two of them are a different category entirely), and build the growth engine that makes the portfolio compound. Brand is the craft; growth is the scoreboard; AI is the force multiplier that lets a lean team punch at agency weight.
 WHAT YOU’LL OWN 
 Codify and defend the voice of each brand — Caregiver at Eden, Creator at Green Room, warm-matriarch at Dulaar, high-energy crowd-pleaser at Backyard Chills, sport-and-community at OneShot Turf — so no two brands collapse into the same voice.
 Own creative direction across outlets — photography, collateral, menus, takeaway, merchandise, in-venue assets — with a standard that survives an expansion to five outlets without a rewrite.
 Architect the annual marketing calendar across dayparts, festivals, launches, and cross-brand plays — VAH Saubhagya hampers, Eden-Dulaar dessert tie-ups, Founder’s Weekend, and Backyard Chills OneShot Turf tournament-and-food combos on match weekends.
 Run the brand book as a living document — evolve it quarterly against guest evidence, not founder opinion.
 Build brand recall that turns each outlet into a destination, not a default — measured in unprompted mention rates, direct-search growth, and organic press coverage.
 Own every platform where the brands appear — District, Zomato, Dineout, Google, Instagram, press, partnerships, plus Playo / Playspots / CricHeroes for OneShot Turf — with a consistent voice per brand and a response SLA no agency currently delivers.
 Evolve packaging and collateral design on a quarterly cadence — menus reprinted against real guest data, takeaway that earns a second photograph, merchandise that regulars actually wear.
 Lead influencer, photographer, event, and PR partner relationships — pick fewer, go deeper, pay on outcomes, not posts.
 WHAT YOU’LL BUILD 
 Transition from the current social media agency to a fully in-house team within 3–6 months — documented handover, zero content drop, cost-per-asset down 40%+.
 Hire and lead an in-house engine: content creator, photographer / videographer, community manager, performance marketer — each role briefed to an output metric, not a task list.
 Operate AI-native across the function — content ideation, creative production, campaign copy, competitive scans, audience research, reporting — so a 4-person team ships what a 10-person agency used to.
 Build a weekly content system across the three brands with a shared production rhythm and three distinct voices — no cross-contamination, no cookie-cutter templating.
 Install a competitive and cultural intelligence loop — F&B trends, platform changes, Kolkata consumer behaviour, national premium benchmarks — read by leadership every Monday.
 WHAT YOU’LL DRIVE 
 Drive performance marketing across Meta, Google, and platform ads — with CAC, ROAS, and incrementality measured brand-by-brand, not pooled.
 Grow organic Instagram by 3x across the portfolio in 12 months — with engagement rate, not follower count, as the real target.
 Launch every outlet campaign with a pre-committed ROI target and a 7-day post-mortem — no campaign gets romanticised after the fact.
 Partner with CRM & Revenue leadership on integrated growth — turning paid acquisition into owned relationships in the guest database.
 WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE (6–12 months) 
 In-house brand and content team fully operational; agency dependency down to zero for BAU.
 Brand books for all five brands (Eden, Green Room, Dulaar, Backyard Chills, OneShot Turf) codified, trained to the floor, and versioned.
 Paid CAC down 30%+ while volume grows; organic social up 3x with rising engagement.
 Every campaign measured, documented, and stored in a repeatable playbook — so outlet #4 launches on muscle memory.
 WHO YOU ARE 
 You have taken a premium consumer brand from mid-awareness to category reference point, and can show the work — the deck, the numbers, the press, the repeat customers.
 You are AI-native — you use modern tools daily for ideation, production, research, and reporting, and you have clear views on which tools earn their place.
 You have hired and led creative and performance talent — photographers, writers, media buyers — and your alumni are themselves in senior roles.
 You hold brand craft and performance rigour in the same hand — you don’t apologise for an ROAS conversation in a brand meeting.
 You have managed a portfolio of sibling brands across different categories or price points without collapsing them into one voice.
 Premium F&B, casual F&B, and sports / lifestyle experience all count — what you’re walking into is a mix of all three, and you’re comfortable toggling between them in the same week.
 REPORTING & STRUCTURE 
 Reports directly to the Founders / Directors. Builds and leads the in-house marketing function — content, performance, community, creative production — across all group brands: Eden Courtyard, The Green Room, Dulaar, Backyard Chills, and OneShot Turf.
 WHAT WE OFFER 
 Competitive and industry-leading compensation, calibrated to the seniority of the mandate. A founder-led group in active expansion that treats brand as the product and growth as the proof. Full creative autonomy to build the function from first principles, with an AI-native operating model from Day 1. A role where the brand book you write gets read — and the campaigns you ship get measured.

title: Lead Demand Generation & Marketing Operations
description: SearchUnify is seeking a Lead Demand Generation & Marketing Operations (710 years) to architect and manage our growth engine. This is a manager-level role for a data-driven leader responsible for leading a team of specialists to drive pipeline, manage high-scale MarTech infrastructure, and optimize marketing spend. As the leader of this function, you are accountable for the entire digital funnel from SEO/AEO and Paid Acquisition to the technical integrity of our Marketo-Zoho integration. We are looking for a strategic powerhouse who can manage people, budgets, and complex data systems with equal proficiency. Key Skills & Qualifications: Experience: 8–10 years in Demand Generation, Digital Marketing, or MarOps (B2B SaaS/IT mandatory). Leadership: Minimum 2 years in a lead or managerial capacity, with a proven ability to manage complex cross-functional projects and stakeholders. MarTech Architecture: Expert-level knowledge of Marketo/Eloqua and Zoho CRM; expert in campaign execution, database segmentation, and technical integrations. Modern Search & Analytics: Deep understanding of the shift from traditional SEO to Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) and the impact of AI on search. Analytical Prowess: Master of Web Analytics (GA4, Clarity); ability to translate behavioral data (bounce rates, session time) into actionable UI/UX improvements. Budget & Forecasting: Expert at "Media Mix Modeling"—allocating spend across channels based on Cost Per Demo (CPD) and ROAS. Data Privacy & Compliance: Deep understanding of 2026 data standards, including first-party data strategies, cookieless tracking, and global compliance (GDPR/CCPA). Experimentation: Experience building a structured A/B testing and CRO culture across entire web ecosystems. Roles and Responsibilities 1. Management & Performance Governance Strategic Oversight: Manage a team of specialists and monitor day-to-day performance of the digital marketing function, ensuring all activities align with quarterly pipeline goals. KPI Reporting: Own Performance Governance—monitoring and reporting on the team’s core KPIs, including demo attribution, organic growth, and ROI. Leadership: Lead internal initiatives and mentor junior team members/associates to ensure high execution standards across the board. 2. Email Marketing & Automation (Marketo) Campaign Execution: Lead the end-to-end launch of email campaigns via Marketo, from audience segmentation to deployment. Performance Optimization: Continuously test and optimize Email Open Rates, Click Rates, and CTOR through A/B testing subject lines, copy, and CTAs. Nurture Strategy: Build and maintain automated nurture streams that move leads through the funnel with minimal manual intervention. SEO, AEO & Web Intelligence Keyword Strategy: Own the Keyword Targeting roadmap. Improve rankings for target keywords in the Top 10 and Top 20 results. Traditional & Technical SEO: Manage technical audits, on-page optimization, and regression monitoring to ensure elite site health and Core Web Vitals. Modern Search (AEO): Optimize content for AI citations and traffic from AI-led search engines Analytics & Behavior: Monitor Website Analytics (Page visits, Bounce rates, LCP) to reduce friction and improve the "Average time spent on page." 4. Paid Acquisition & Budgeting Media Mix: Determine the best allocation of the marketing budget ($) across Google Ads, LinkedIn, and Content Syndication based on Cost Per Demo. Conversion Rate Optimization: Lead an experimentation framework to improve landing page conversions and paid campaign efficiency. 5. Brand Advocacy & Share of Voice (SoV) Employee Advocacy: Orchestrate Employee-Led Social Posting programs to amplify brand reach and improve organic social impressions. Share of Voice: Monitor and report on Share of Voice (SoV) for targeted keywords to ensure SearchUnify remains dominant against competitors. Community Engagement: Oversee strategic outreach on Reddit and LinkedIn Groups to drive value-add conversations. 6. MarTech Governance & Compliance Stack Integrity: Ensure Chatbots, Clarity, Marketo, and Zoho are perfectly synced with zero data loss. Data Privacy: Manage first-party data strategies and ensure compliance with 2026 global privacy standards (GDPR/CCPA/Cookieless).

 Positioning & messaging

 Keep differentiation sharp vs incumbents and modern alternatives

 Research & insights

 Refine ICPs, use cases, and packaging inputs based on evidence
 Feed insights back into roadmap conversations with Product
 Enablement (Sales, Partners, Community) 
 Create sales collateral: decks, one-pagers, demo scripts, talk tracks, FAQs
 Arm teams with objection handling and competitive angles that match reality
 Produce edition explainers (cloud vs self-hosted vs air-gapped)
 Keep the enablement library current and usable
 Website & product storytelling 
 Own copy for product pages, solution pages, industry pages, and comparison pages
 Turn complex features into clear “what it is / who it’s for / why it matters” narratives
 Improve evaluation flow: faster understanding, fewer unanswered questions
 Ensure consistency across web, docs, and launch surfaces
 Adoption & lifecycle 
 Partner with Growth to improve activation and feature adoption
 Define adoption moments and build lifecycle messaging to get users there
 Support onboarding narratives by persona and use case
 Reduce churn drivers through clearer value reinforcement
 Measurement & iteration 
 Define success metrics for launches and messaging changes
 Track adoption signals, conversion impact, and pipeline influence
 Use feedback (support, sales calls, community) to refine positioning
 Iterate fast: ship, measure, adjust, repeat
 Qualifications (must-haves) 
 4–8 years in product marketing for B2B SaaS.
 Proven ability to build positioning and messaging that drives adoption and revenue.
 Strong writing: you can ship web-ready copy and launch assets, not just strategy docs.
 Experience leading cross-functional launches end-to-end.
 Comfort with technical audiences (developers/IT) and enterprise buyers.
 Ability to use data and research to make decisions (quant + qual).
 Strong execution in an async, fast-moving environment.
 Bonus points 
 Open-source / open-core, self-hosted, or multi-edition SaaS experience.
 Familiarity with enterprise/security/compliance contexts (SSO, audit, deployment constraints, air-gapped requirements).
 Experience with partner-led distribution or community-driven GTM.
 Strong competitive instincts in crowded categories (work management, devtools, collaboration).

title: Product Marketing Manager
description:
About eG Innovations 
 eG Innovations builds full-stack observability software for enterprise IT teams. Our flagship product, eG Enterprise, supports over 650 technologies across applications, infrastructure, cloud, containers, virtual machines, databases, and digital workspaces through a single platform.
 The product is built for IT teams running complex, hybrid environments that don't want to stitch together five different tools to figure out why something is slow. AI-driven correlation, auto-baselining, and root-cause analysis are at the core of how it works. It's available as SaaS or on-premises and recognised as an APM Champion by Software Reviews two years running.
 We've been at this since 2001. The product is used across healthcare (Radboudumc, Ziekenhuis Gelderse Vallei), retail and logistics (eBay, Dachser), manufacturing (kt&G), finance, education, and government, with customers and teams across India, the US, Singapore, Middle East and Europe.
 We compete with Datadog, Dynatrace, New Relic, and SolarWinds in the broader APM and monitoring market, and with Citrix Director, Control Up, Nexthink, and Lakeside on the digital workspace side.
 About this role 
 A buyer comparing eG against Datadog or Nexthink should land on a page that meets them at their intent. This role builds out that surface — comparison pages, solution pages, industry BOFU pages, and the messaging architecture that ties them together — at the depth and pace our pipeline goals require.
 You'll work with the Web Growth & Conversion team, an established demand gen function, and an SEO agency that's been briefed but is hungry for sharper commercial-intent direction.
 The role spans Digital Workspace Monitoring (Citrix, Omnissa Horizon, Microsoft AVD, Amazon Workspaces, DEX), APM, infrastructure monitoring, and SAP monitoring as a focused commercial priority over the next 12 months.
 The Mandate 
 Connect product truth to buyer language — own content, messaging, and sales enablement as commercial outputs that drive pipeline.
 What you'll own 
 Content production: You own the content roadmap, and you write it. Comparison pages against Datadog, Nexthink, SolarWinds, Azure Monitor, and Dynatrace. Solution pages covering Citrix Monitoring, AVD Monitoring, SAP Monitoring, and DEX. Industry BOFU pages for BFSI, Healthcare, and Manufacturing. A blog refresh programme tied to commercial-intent keywords. The best comparison pages we admire treat the competitor honestly and win on the surfaces that matter to the buyer — that's the bar.
 Messaging architecture: You maintain the positioning framework that every page, ad, pitch deck, and SEO brief flows from. You write and own the messaging house. When the sales pitch, the website hero, and the paid ad each tell a different story about who eG serves and why, you fix it — and you build the system that prevents it recurring. ICP discipline sits with you.
 Sales enablement: You build battle cards for the displacement conversations sales has every week — Datadog, Nexthink, SolarWinds, Azure Monitor. Vertical one-pagers for BFSI, Healthcare, and Manufacturing. Demo support materials mapped to where deals actually stall. Success looks like AEs reaching for your assets in live calls, not slides sitting unopened in a shared drive.
 The content-to-SEO pipeline: You work with the Web Growth team and the SEO agency to convert keyword intent into published, ranking, converting pages. Brief, draft, publish, track. You'll hold the agency to pipeline outcomes, not rankings. The comparison page architecture is your 90-day proof point and the template for everything that follows.
 What you'll need 
 Hands-on experience with:
 AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, or similar) as a content production system — building structured workflows and prompts that produce publication-ready, on-brand output, not iterative prompt ping-pong
 A CMS — WordPress, Web flow, or equivalent — comfortable shipping pages yourself
 SEO toolkit — Ahrefs or SEMrush, with a working grasp of Search Console and the difference between commercial and informational intent
 A content workflow tool — Notion, Asana, or equivalent — for managing a multi-asset pipeline
 Google Analytics and basic attribution — enough to read what your content is doing post-publish
 Background: 
 4 to 8 years total experience in B2B SaaS product marketing or commercial content marketing
 Personally written and published comparison pages, solution pages, and BOFU content — not briefed an agency to do it
 Fluent with messaging framework — you've contributed to or owned the building of one, and you can articulate the choices that went into it
 Shipped sales enablement material — battle cards, displacement decks, vertical one-pagers 
 Nice to have, not required: 
 HubSpot or equivalent CRM familiarity — understands how content feeds the MQL pipeline
 Exposure to IT infrastructure, observability, monitoring, DevOps, or enterprise software
 An instinct for how CIOs, IT Directors, and IT Managers buy
 Prior experience directing an external SEO or content agency
 The first 90 days 
 We'll agree on your success criteria together before you join. Broadly, by Day 90 you'll have:
 Completed a messaging audit — how eG is described on the website, how sales pitch it, and what buyers are searching for — with a gap report and specific recommendations, not observations
 Shipped the eG vs Datadog comparison page live, with the architecture in place for the rest of the competitor set
 Battle cards for the top two competitive displacement scenarios in seller hands and being referenced in calls
 Three solution pages revised, the BFSI industry page live, and messaging house v1 documented and circulated to sales, product, and agency partners
 What we'll ask you to show us in interviews 
 A piece of content you wrote personally — comparison page, solution page, or BOFU asset — and the brief, intent, and pipeline outcome behind it. We're more interested in "we wrote a comparison page targeting bottom-funnel intent, captured X demos, closed Y deals" than "we hit 10k visits."
 A walkthrough of how you use AI tools in your content workflow — the structure, not just the prompt. Show us the system, not the chat log.
 Who this role isn't for 
 If you measure your work in reach, impressions, and brand rather than pipeline contribution, the bar here will feel uncomfortable
 If your background is D2C, e-commerce, or consumer brand work, the mechanics don't transfer to enterprise IT buying cycles
 If your content experience is mostly agency coordination with little personal output to point to, this isn't that role — you'll be in the work
 If you call yourself an SEO expert but can't articulate the difference between commercial-intent and informational-intent search, you'll struggle here

description:
Role Overview
 The B2C Product Marketing Manager is a senior individual contributor based in Optum India, operating as an integrated extension of the US product marketing team. The role supports consumer and provider-facing pharmacy products and enhancements across digital, retail, and care channels and functionally reports to a US-based Director, Product Marketing. The PMM develops positioning, messaging, and naming; creates product and product-family materials; and drives disciplined go-to-market execution for offerings such as digital pharmacy features, mail service and specialty pharmacy programs, affordability tools, adherence initiatives, and provider enablement. The role demands strong judgment, executive-ready communication, and comfort in a matrixed, regulated PBM environment (with legal/compliance review and accessibility standards), while working with minimal guidance.
 Reporting & Governance
 Functional reporting to Director, Product Marketing.
 Day-to-day priorities and performance aligned to US product marketing roadmaps.
 Embedded offshore role; not a shared services or agency model.
 Evaluated on business outcomes (quality, impact, adoption), not volume of output.
 Key Responsibilities
 Product Strategy, Positioning & Messaging: 
 Develop value-based positioning, messaging, and naming for consumer/provider products focused on affordability, access, adherence, and experience.
 Build and maintain messaging frameworks and product-family narratives that stay consistent across channels (web/app, CRM/email/SMS, IVR/call scripts, pharmacy counter materials). 
 Go-to-Market & Launch Execution: 
 Lead launch planning and readiness; coordinate product, clinical, legal/compliance, brand, UX/content design, digital, CX, sales/account teams.
 Manage timelines, asset creation and approvals, and enablement for digital feature releases, program rollouts, and communications (e.g., formulary changes, prior authorization updates). 
 Enablement & Content Systems: 
 Create and maintain consumer-facing materials (one-pagers, landing-page copy, app store descriptions, FAQs) and provider-facing talking points.
 Develop executive presentations and standardized content libraries for reuse across campaigns, channels, and product families. 
 Stakeholder Partnership & Compliance: 
 Assess stakeholder needs, translate ambiguity into structured outputs, and propose solutions to nonstandard requests.
 Ensure all materials meet legal/regulatory and brand guidelines; apply accessibility and plain-language standards; substantiate claims with conservative proof-points.
 Analytics & Optimization: 
 Partner on measurement plans (activation, first fill, refill adherence, CTR/engagement, CSAT/NPS) and incorporate A/B learnings to improve clarity and conversion.
 Required Qualifications
 Bachelors degree in communications, public relations, marketing, business, or related field; or equivalent experience.
 Overall 10 -12 years of experience with 5+ years in communications/marketing and 5+ years project management in a matrix organization.
 5+ years professional writing/editing; 
 Demonstrated ability to clarify, manage, and prioritize multiple deliverables; proven organizational skills.
 Healthcare/PBM experience preferred; consumer marketing in regulated environments a strong plus.

We are seeking a talented and experienced Marketing Manager to join our team. As a Marketing Manager for our AI SaaS products, you will be responsible for overseeing branding communications and implementing various marketing strategies, including digital marketing, email marketing, social media marketing, LinkedIn marketing, website marketing, SEO, SEM, and more. Your role will be instrumental in driving brand awareness, generating leads, and achieving marketing objectives for our innovative AI-driven pricing solutions.
 Responsibilities 
- Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies to promote our AI SaaS products for dynamic pricing, ensuring brand consistency and market positioning.
- Oversee and coordinate branding communications, including messaging, visual identity, and tone of voice, to create a compelling and cohesive brand image.
- Plan, execute, and optimize digital marketing campaigns across various channels, including email marketing, social media marketing, LinkedIn marketing, and website marketing.
- Collaborate with the content team to create engaging and informative content that resonates with our target audience, including blog posts, whitepapers, case studies, and more.
- Implement effective SEO strategies to improve organic search rankings and increase website traffic, ensuring visibility and discoverability of our products and solutions.
- Manage paid advertising campaigns, including search engine marketing (SEM), display advertising, and retargeting, to drive qualified traffic and generate leads.
- Monitor and analyze marketing metrics, including website traffic, conversion rates, and campaign performance, to make data-driven decisions and optimize marketing initiatives.
- Collaborate with the sales team to develop marketing collateral, sales enablement materials, and presentations that effectively communicate the value proposition of our AI SaaS products.
- Stay up-to-date with industry trends, competitive landscape, and emerging technologies in AI and dynamic pricing, incorporating relevant insights into marketing strategies.
- Manage and build relationships with external partners, agencies, and vendors to support marketing initiatives and ensure effective execution of campaigns.
 Qualifications 
- Proven experience as a Marketing Manager, preferably in the AI or SaaS industry, with a strong focus on digital marketing and branding communications.
- In-depth knowledge of marketing strategies and tactics, including email marketing, social media marketing, LinkedIn marketing, website marketing, SEO, and SEM.
- Familiarity with AI concepts and technologies, particularly in the field of dynamic pricing, is highly desirable.
- Demonstrated ability to develop and execute successful marketing campaigns, driving brand awareness, lead generation, and customer acquisition.
- Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions to optimize marketing efforts.
- Proficiency in using marketing automation tools, CRM systems, and digital analytics platforms.
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to create compelling content and effectively communicate ideas.
- Strong project management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks, meet deadlines, and manage multiple projects simultaneously.
- Creative mindset with the ability to think strategically and generate innovative marketing ideas.
- Master's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field. Additional certifications in digital marketing or AI would be a plus.
If you are a strategic and results-oriented Marketing Manager with a passion for AI SaaS products and dynamic pricing, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your resume and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience and explaining why you are the ideal candidate for this position.

title: Product & Content Marketing Manager
description:
Job Title: Manager Marketing (Product & Content Marketing) 
 Location: Gurugram, Haryana
 Experience: Atleast 10 Yrs
 About Us 
 Soprema Private Limited is part of the global SOPREMA Group, a world leader in waterproofing, thermal insulation, and soundproofing solutions for building envelopes and civil engineering sectors. Founded in 1908, SOPREMA operates in over 100 countries with a global turnover of 5.5 billion (2025).
 In India, SOPREMA has been present since 2017, delivering high-performance waterproofing systems across major infrastructure projects such as airports, tunnels, and commercial developments. The company is driven by innovation, quality solutions, and strong customer service.
 Job Summary 
 We are seeking a dynamic and technically inclined Marketing Manager to lead product-focused marketing initiatives for our organization. The ideal candidate will have a strong ability to understand industrial/technical products, translate product features into compelling value propositions, and drive targeted communication across digital and traditional marketing channels. This role bridges product knowledge and marketing execution creating content and campaigns for specific customer segments including contractors, consultants, specifiers, government bodies, and end users.
 Key Responsibilities 
 Product Understanding & Positioning 
 Develop deep understanding of the product portfolio — technical specifications, applications, competitive advantages, and end-use segments
 Work closely with the sales, technical, and R&D teams to translate product knowledge into clear, audience-specific messaging
 Create and maintain product literature, datasheets, application guides, and case studies
 Content Development & Marketing 
 Develop a content calendar and drive content creation across formats — articles, videos, brochures, presentations, emailers, and social media posts
 Ensure all content is accurate, technically sound, and aligned with brand guidelines
 Develop segment-specific content for different audiences (e.g., infrastructure consultants, government bodies, EPC contractors)
 Digital Marketing Execution 
 Manage and drive the company's presence on LinkedIn, YouTube, and other relevant platforms
 Oversee website content updates, email marketing campaigns, and basic SEO hygiene
 Track digital engagement metrics and optimize content strategy based on performance data
 Coordinate with digital agencies or in-house teams for campaign execution
 Traditional Marketing 
 Plan and execute participation in trade fairs, industry exhibitions, and technical seminars
 Manage print collaterals, product catalogues, and mailers for field sales support
 Coordinate with PR agencies for press releases, industry publications, and editorial features
 Market Intelligence & Segment Targeting 
 Identify key customer segments and develop targeted outreach strategies
 Gather competitor intelligence and market feedback to refine positioning and messaging
 Support the sales team with marketing tools, pitch decks, and customer-facing materials
 Brand Building & Thought Leadership 
 Build the company's brand as a knowledge leader in the industry through white papers, webinars, and technical articles
 Develop relationships with industry bodies, associations, and key publications
 Required Skills 
 Strong ability to understand technical/industrial products and simplify them for varied audiences
 Proficiency in content creation — written, visual, and video formats
 Working knowledge of digital platforms: LinkedIn, YouTube, email marketing tools, basic SEO
 Experience with traditional marketing tools: exhibitions, catalogues, PR, print media
 Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills
 Data-oriented mindset with ability to measure and report marketing effectiveness
 Qualifications 
 Bachelor's or Master's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Engineering (with MBA), or related field
 8–10 years of experience in B2B or industrial product marketing
 Experience in construction, building materials, geosynthetics, or infrastructure sector preferred
